snippets.title: Madhu Guru on models and product
snippets.takeaway: Having been through many frontier model launch reviews, I have empathy for everyone involved. Launching an LLM isn't like shipping traditional software - you're making a decision...
snippets.summary: Madhu Guru (realmadhuguru on X) shared 1 recent update. Having been through many frontier model launch reviews, I have empathy for everyone involved. Launching an LLM isn't like shipping traditional software - you're making a decision about a black box with effectively infinite use cases and infinite failure modes. The tradeoffs are hard - every increase in capability expands the space of both valuable use cases and potential misuse.

snippets.title: Aaron Levie on models and costs
snippets.takeaway: This whole Fable export control situation is actually net positive to regulation discourse. It’s an early peek into what AI regulation would end up looking like at scale when...
snippets.summary: Aaron Levie (levie on X) shared 3 recent updates. This whole Fable export control situation is actually net positive to regulation discourse. It’s an early peek into what AI regulation would end up looking like at scale when enacted at the model layer instead of the specific application of the AI. The government would have sole discretion over when a model can be released to the to public, based on a bunch of factors that they inherently control.

snippets.title: Garry Tan on models
snippets.takeaway: In AI most people are still trying to use old maps on a new territory. Throw the maps away. It's time to draw new ones. The only way you can do it is walking the land.
snippets.summary: Garry Tan (garrytan on X) shared 2 recent updates. In AI most people are still trying to use old maps on a new territory. Throw the maps away. It's time to draw new ones. The only way you can do it is walking the land. I wish the significance of the model came from more people actually using it and coming to their own conclusions But yes most people in the world learn about it through signifiers and not through interacting with that which is signified

snippets.title: Aditya Agarwal on ai
snippets.takeaway: I have been thinking about this photo a lot. It's a beautiful testament to human civilization and our ability to reach for the stars.
snippets.summary: Aditya Agarwal (adityaag on X) shared 2 recent updates. I have been thinking about this photo a lot. It's a beautiful testament to human civilization and our ability to reach for the stars. But the thing that I can't get over is that a *human being* took this photo. From a tin cylinder flying on the other side of the moon. A machine could have taken it and it would have still been cool. But man was there. And that makes it so much more evocative.

snippets.title: Biohub: The Future of Biology is Open-Source with Co-Founders Mark Zuckerberg, Priscilla Chan, and Head of Science Alex Rives
snippets.takeaway: Enterprise AI is moving from chat rollout toward agentic workflows, with token budgets, rollout speed, and workflow integration becoming the practical bottlenecks.
snippets.summary: I think one of the really interesting things that we've been seeing is people kind of connecting it with AgenTex systems to just kind of do automated design and kind of just automate that whole process. And yet, you know, the AI scientists that work here could also go work in commercial enterprises. And, you know, we're in the age of information theory at scale, and we have these systems that can basically kind of predict the next token, and they can, learn world models from that.
